perturbation.original_text: 1. The term “ Confidential Information ” shall mean any confidential, proprietary, financial or otherwise non- public information, in written or electronic form, relating to the Disclosing Party, or t he Purpose that is disclosed before, on or after the Effective Date, by the Disclosing Party to the Recipient or its affiliates, or to any of such Recipient’s or its affiliates’ employees, officers, directors, partners, shareholders, agents, attorneys, accountants or advisors (collectively, “ Representatives” ), whether disclosed or accessed in written, electronic or other form, including, without limitation: (a) information concerning the Disclosing Party’s and its affiliates’ business affairs, finances, for ecasts, sales and other financial results, records and budgets, and business, marketing, development, sales and other commercial strategies; and (b) notes, analyses, compilations, reports, forecasts, data, statistics, summaries, interpretations and other m aterials prepared by or for Recipient or its Representatives that contain, are based on, or otherwise reflect or are derived, in whole or in part, from any of the foregoing. Confidential Information provided by Disclosing Party must be expressly marked or designated in writing by the Disclosing Party in a manner to indicate its confidential, proprietary or otherwise non- public nature.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. The term “ Confidential Information ” shall mean any information, in written or electronic form, relating to the Disclosing Party, or t he Purpose that is disclosed before, on or after the Effective Date, by the Disclosing Party to the Recipient or its affiliates, or to any of such Recipient’s or its affiliates’ employees, officers, directors, partners, shareholders, agents, attorneys, accountants or advisors (collectively, “ Representatives” ), whether disclosed or accessed in written, electronic or other form, including, without limitation: (a) information concerning the Disclosing Party’s and its affiliates’ business affairs, finances, for ecasts, sales and other financial results, records and budgets, and business, marketing, development, sales and other commercial strategies; and (b) notes, analyses, compilations, reports, forecasts, data, statistics, summaries, interpretations and other m aterials prepared by or for Recipient or its Representatives that contain, are based on, or otherwise reflect or are derived, in whole or in part, from any of the foregoing. Confidential Information provided by Disclosing Party must be expressly marked or designated in writing by the Disclosing Party in a manner to indicate its confidential, proprietary or otherwise non- public nature.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words "confidential, proprietary, financial or otherwise non- public" from the definition of "Confidential Information", it creates uncertainty as to what constitutes confidential information, specifically contradicting section 7 which excludes information already publicly available. Now, all information relating to the Disclosing party would be considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall not include any information which (a) is or becomes publicly available, other than through di sclosure by Recipient in violation of this Agreement, (b) was known or available to Recipient prior to disclosure pursuant hereto, (c) was independently developed by Recipient without reference to the Confidential Information disclosed to it pursuant heret o, or (d) in the future is received from a third party who, to the knowledge of Recipient at the time of disclosure, is under no obligation of confidentiality with respect thereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' as 'confidential, proprietary, financial or otherwise non- public information'. Section 7 explicitly excludes publicly available information from this definition. By removing the words 'confidential, proprietary, financial or otherwise non- public' from the definition in the changed text, it broadens the scope of 'Confidential Information' to include *any* information relating to the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts Section 7, because now even publicly available information relating to the Disclosing Party would be considered 'Confidential Information', which is a clear inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Recipient shall not, and s hall cause its Representatives not to, (a) disclose all or any portion of Confidential Information to any third parties (other than to its Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information to assist Recipient in its evaluation of the Purpose ), or (b) use or permit any Confidential Information to be accessed or used for any purpose other than for the evalua tion of the Purpose . Recipient will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement caused by its Representatives. Notwithstandi ng anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Disclosing Party acknowledges and agrees that (a) the Recipient is expressly authorized to disclose the Confidential Information to certain lenders and investors, in furtherance of Recipient’s perfor mance of its obligations with respect to the Purpose and (b) under no circumstances will Recipient have any liability for any disclosure by such lenders or investors of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Recipient shall not disclose all or any portion of Confidential Information to any third parties (other than to its Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information to assist Recipient in its evaluation of the Purpose ), or (b) use or permit any Confidential Information to be accessed or used for any purpose other than for the evalua tion of the Purpose . Recipient will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement caused by its Representatives. Notwithstandi ng anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Disclosing Party acknowledges and agrees that (a) the Recipient is expressly authorized to disclose the Confidential Information to certain lenders and investors, in furtherance of Recipient’s perfor mance of its obligations with respect to the Purpose and (b) under no circumstances will Recipient have any liability for any disclosure by such lenders or investors of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "shall cause its Representatives not to" creates an in-text contradiction within Section 3. The Recipient is not allowed to disclose Confidential Information to any third parties, yet Representatives can disclose Confidential Information, creating conflicting stipulations within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Recipient shall not disclose all or any portion of Confidential Information to any third parties (other than to its Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information to assist Recipient in its evaluation of the Purpose ), or (b) use or permit any Confidential Information to be accessed or used for any purpose other than for the evalua tion of the Purpose . Recipient will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement caused by its Representatives. Notwithstandi ng anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Disclosing Party acknowledges and agrees that (a) the Recipient is expressly authorized to disclose the Confidential Information to certain lenders and investors, in furtherance of Recipient’s perfor mance of its obligations with respect to the Purpose and (b) under no circumstances will Recipient have any liability for any disclosure by such lenders or investors of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Recipient shall not, and shall cause its Representatives not to' disclose confidential information, directly obligating the Recipient to prevent their Representatives from disclosing it. The changed text removes the phrase 'shall cause its Representatives not to,' creating a situation where the Recipient is prohibited from disclosing the information, but the Representatives are not explicitly bound by the same restriction. This creates a conflict within Section 3, as the Recipient's Representatives can disclose confidential information, while the Recipient is restricted from doing so.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Recipient hereby further agrees that, in the event of its breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party would suffer irreparable harm and the Disclosing Party’s remedies shall include, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, equitable remedies such as specific performance and injunct ive relief and shall not be limited to monetary damages. Specifically, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief prohibiting such violation.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Recipient hereby further agrees that, in the event of its breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party would suffer irreparable harm and the Disclosing Party’s remedies shall include, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, equitable remedies such as specific performance and shall not be limited to monetary damages. Specifically, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief prohibiting such violation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'injunctive relief' from the general remedies, while still stating the Disclosing party is entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief, contradicts the scope of available remedies. It creates an in-text contradiction about the availablility of equitable remedies outside the immediate injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Recipient hereby further agrees that, in the event of its breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party would suffer irreparable harm and the Disclosing Party’s remedies shall include, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity, equitable remedies such as specific performance and shall not be limited to monetary damages. Specifically, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief prohibiting such violation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'injunctive relief' from the list of general equitable remedies available to the Disclosing Party while simultaneously stating that the Disclosing Party is entitled to seek 'immediate injunctive relief'. This creates a contradiction: the general remedies clause suggests injunctive relief might not be available as a general remedy, while the subsequent sentence explicitly states it is available in a specific circumstance. This inconsistency about the scope of available remedies constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, which matches the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction perturbation type, and directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text itself.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Neither Party may assign, transfer or sell any of its rights under this Agreement, or delegate any of its obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party. Each Party understands and agrees that no failure or delay by the other in exercising any of its rights or remedies hereunder will operate as a waiver thereof, nor will any waiver in any instance constitute a waiver with respect to any other instance.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Neither Party may assign, transfer or sell any of its rights under this Agreement, or delegate any of its obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party. Each Party understands and agrees that no failure or delay by the other in exercising any of its rights or remedies hereunder will operate as a waiver thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "nor will any waiver in any instance constitute a waiver with respect to any other instance" from the original text now it creates an in-text contradiction in Section 10 of the contract related to waiver. The remaining part of the paragraph states that a failure or delay in exercising rights doesn't act as a waiver, but now there is no specification that a waiver in one situation doesn't apply to others. Therefore, it opens the door that each waiver constitutes a waiver to any instance within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Neither Party may assign, transfer or sell any of its rights under this Agreement, or delegate any of its obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party. Each Party understands and agrees that no failure or delay by the other in exercising any of its rights or remedies hereunder will operate as a waiver thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a clause ensuring that a waiver in one instance doesn't automatically apply to all other instances. The changed text removes this clause. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The explanation states that removing this clause opens the door to interpreting a waiver as applying to all instances, creating a contradiction within Section 10 regarding the scope and effect of waivers. Thus, the change causes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Upon request of the Disclosing Party (which request may be made at any time upon reasonable notice), Recipient will destroy (or, if stored electronically, purge) or deliver to the Disclosing Party all copies of written Confidential Information (except for that portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilation, or other documents prepared by Recipient) in Recipient’s possession. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilations and other documents prepared by Recipient will continue to be subj ect to the terms of this Agreement or shall be destroyed (or, if stored electronically, purged). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient may retain copies of Confidential Information that it reasonably believes it is required to retain under applicable laws, banking regulations and internal policies of which relate to any services provided to the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall terminate upon the earliest of (i) one (1) year from the date hereof, (ii) the date all Confidential Information is destroy ed by Recipient or returned to the Disclosing Party, or (iii) upon the closing of any transaction between Recipient and - 3 - the Disclosing Party for credit accommodations or banking services, at which time duties of confidentiality shall be determined by the t ransaction agreements and applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Upon request of the Disclosing Party (which request may be made at any time upon reasonable notice), Recipient will destroy (or, if stored electronically, purge) or deliver to the Disclosing Party all copies of written Confidential Information in Recipient’s possession. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilations and other documents prepared by Recipient will continue to be subj ect to the terms of this Agreement or shall be destroyed (or, if stored electronically, purged). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient may retain copies of Confidential Information that it reasonably believes it is required to retain under applicable laws, banking regulations and internal policies of which relate to any services provided to the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall terminate upon the earliest of (i) one (1) year from the date hereof, (ii) the date all Confidential Information is destroy ed by Recipient or returned to the Disclosing Party, or (iii) upon the closing of any transaction between Recipient and - 3 - the Disclosing Party for credit accommodations or banking services, at which time duties of confidentiality shall be determined by the t ransaction agreements and applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "(except for that portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilation, or other documents prepared by Recipient)" it creates an in-text contradiction with the very next sentence, which states 'That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilations and other documents prepared by Recipient will continue to be subject to the terms of this Agreement or shall be destroyed (or, if stored electronically, purged).'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Upon request of the Disclosing Party (which request may be made at any time upon reasonable notice), Recipient will destroy (or, if stored electronically, purge) or deliver to the Disclosing Party all copies of written Confidential Information in Recipient’s possession. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilations and other documents prepared by Recipient will continue to be subj ect to the terms of this Agreement or shall be destroyed (or, if stored electronically, purged). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient may retain copies of Confidential Information that it reasonably believes it is required to retain under applicable laws, banking regulations and internal policies of which relate to any services provided to the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall terminate upon the earliest of (i) one (1) year from the date hereof, (ii) the date all Confidential Information is destroy ed by Recipient or returned to the Disclosing Party, or (iii) upon the closing of any transaction between Recipient and - 3 - the Disclosing Party for credit accommodations or banking services, at which time duties of confidentiality shall be determined by the t ransaction agreements and applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11 removes the exception for Confidential Information found in analysis or compilations, but the very next sentence still refers to 'That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analysis, compilations and other documents prepared by Recipient.' This creates a direct contradiction because the first part of the changed text implies all written Confidential Information must be destroyed/returned, while the subsequent sentence contradicts that by discussing the handling of Confidential Information in analyses and compilations. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as a clause is placed with conflicting implications.
